title: ultra tender oaxacan meat barbacoa style casserole
description: a briny, aerated, mexican (oaxacan) dish featuring caramelized sugar sauce. this casserole is perfect for a solo dinner.
instructions: season the beef brisket with ancho chili powder, cumin, and salt.
heat the lard or oil in a large dutch oven over medium-high heat. sear the beef brisket on all sides, then transfer to a plate and set aside.
reduce heat to medium and add the onion and garlic to the pot. cook, stirring occasionally, until the onion is softened and caramelized, about 20 minutes.
add the chipotle peppers in adobo to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
add the bay leaves and meat broth to the pot and stir to combine. bring the mixture to a simmer.

place the seared beef brisket on top of the onion mixture in the pot. cover the pot with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven. cook for 2 hours and 30 minutes, or until the beef is tender and easily shreds with a fork.
remove the pot from the oven and carefully transfer the beef and onion mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish. sprinkle the sugar over the top of the beef.
transfer the baking dish to the oven and cook for an additional 15 minutes, or until the sugar is caramelized and the beef is crispy on top.
remove the casserole from the oven and sprinkle with chopped cilantro. serve hot.
